qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SSRF in GraphQL asset upload allows internal network access
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes SQL Injection via criteria[orderBy] in the element-indexes/get-elements endpoint (CWE-89). This finding describes SSRF via hostname validation bypass in the GraphQL asset upload mutation in validateHostname() at src/gql/resolvers/mutations/Asset.php:281-319. Different vulnerable function, different endpoint, different vulnerability class, different root cause entirely.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory (GHSA-2453-mppf-46cj) describes SQL Injection via unsanitized criteria[orderBy] input in the element-indexes/get-elements endpoint of Craft CMS (CWE-89). The only qualifying finding (graphql_ssrf_asset_upload) describes SSRF via hostname validation bypass in a different endpoint (GraphQL asset upload) and different function (validateHostname). No finding matches the advisory root cause: unsanitized ORDER BY clause construction from user-controlled criteria[orderBy] in the element index controller.
evidence: Advisory root cause: SQL Injection via criteria[orderBy] parameter in POST to element-indexes/get-elements, lacking sanitization before use in ORDER BY clause
graphql_ssrf_asset_upload root cause: Insufficient hostname validation in validateHostname() at src/gql/resolvers/mutations/Asset.php:281-319, allowing hostnames that resolve to private IPs
